name: Rebuff API
what_it_does: Open-source self-hardening prompt injection detection library using a multi-layer defense combining heuristics, vector similarity, LLM analysis, and canary tokens.
use_cases: Detect and block prompt injection attempts in cost-sensitive agent pipelines without paid API costs
Self-harden detection over time using canary tokens to identify novel injection patterns
Run fully on-premises prompt injection defense with no data leaving the environment
Integrate with existing LangChain or custom agent orchestration as a pre-filter
Evaluate and compare open-source prompt injection defenses before committing to paid solutions
not_for: Production pipelines requiring enterprise SLA or guaranteed uptime — hosted API is community-maintained
Teams needing out-of-the-box accuracy comparable to commercial solutions like Lakera Guard
best_when: Best when cost is the primary constraint, data cannot leave the environment, or you need a self-hostable open-source prompt injection defense that improves through use.
avoid_when: Avoid when detection accuracy is critical and budget allows for commercial alternatives, or when you need a fully managed service with SLA guarantees.

auth.notes: API key required for the hosted Rebuff API. Self-hosted deployments can run without authentication or implement custom auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free and open source (MIT license); hosted API available with community-managed limits
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers — self-host for production use; hosted API is for evaluation only
pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Self-hosting requires a Pinecone account (for vector similarity layer) and an OpenAI API key (for LLM analysis layer).

performance.rate_limits: No documented rate limits for self-hosted; hosted API is community-maintained with no formal limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error handling is functional but basic — community project with limited investment in developer experience polish.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Detection requests are stateless; same input always produces same result (deterministic layers) or near-same (LLM layer may vary slightly).
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Pinecone and OpenAI API keys for full multi-layer functionality — adds external dependencies
LLM analysis layer introduces variable latency and cost depending on OpenAI pricing
False positive rate is higher than commercial alternatives — threshold tuning required
Canary token self-hardening requires persistent state across requests — needs careful setup in stateless environments
Hosted API is not production-grade — intended for evaluation only

security_score_components.security_notes: Open source allows full code audit. No compliance certifications. Self-hosted deployments inherit the security posture of the host environment.
